Sunburst 417-284-3443 / Dawt Mill 417-284-3540) and access-point GPS needs human pin placement (only Tecumseh has a coordinate).
name: North Fork River
description: The North Fork of the White River, a classic spring-fed south-central Missouri float and wild-trout stream fed by Rainbow and Blue Springs. Clear, cold, and reliably floatable; small ledges/rapids.
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (state-managed accesses); USFS (Mark Twain NF) and USACE (Norfork Lake) at some points; streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

gauges.name: North Fork River near Tecumseh, MO
gauges.knownBias: at the end of the run (mile 49.1, The Forks); over-reads the upper reach

sections.name: North Fork main reach (SH 14 → Tecumseh)
sections.description: The popular lower/main reach: SH 14 bridge (Twin Bridges, mile 24.1) down to Tecumseh / Norfork Lake (mile 49.5) — the outfitter-hub stretch and the most-floated water on the North Fork. Clear, cold, spring-fed Class I–II with a 3-ft ledge (The Falls, mile 36.9) and the Dawt Mill low-head dam (mile 45.6); the lowest ~2 miles below The Forks are Norfork Lake backwater. The Tecumseh gauge (07057500) sits at the downstream end of the reach (mile 49.1) and over-reads the thinner water upstream.

representativeGauge.rationale: 07057500 (North Fork River near Tecumseh) is the only pollable USGS gauge on the North Fork, and it is the gauge the rivers.moherp.org OBSERVED community ratings are keyed to. It sits at the downstream end of this reach (mile 49.1, The Forks).
representativeGauge.knownBias: at the end of the run (mile 49.1); over-reads the upper reach — the calibration fits this lower/main reach, and water upstream runs thinner than the number suggests

localKnowledge: Blue Ribbon wild rainbow trout fishery — one of Missouri's premier trout streams; strongly spring-fed (Blue, North Fork, Rainbow/Double, and Althea springs) keeps it clear, cold, and floatable near year-round.

The Tecumseh gauge (07057500) sits at the downstream end of the run (mile 49.1, The Forks) and OVER-READS the upper reach — the badge calibration fits this lower/main reach; upstream water runs thinner than the number suggests.

Three-part reach structure (owner river-guide): remote, strainer-heavy headwaters above SH 76 (~28.5 mi); scenic, lightly-used middle SH 76 → SH 14 (~24.1 mi); and the most-popular, crowded lower reach SH 14 → Tecumseh (~25.4 mi).

The lowest ~2 miles (below The Forks / mile 49.1 down to Tecumseh) are Norfork Lake backwater — flat lakewater that must be paddled; the Tecumseh access was rebuilt after the 2017 flood and is often submerged by high lake level (park on the Hwy 160 shoulder and carry).
Hebron / 'Steel Bridge' access (mile 18.7): repeated car break-in / crime reports — leave no valuables in vehicles.

Five outfitters serve the river — Twin Bridges (mile 24.1), River of Life Farm (mile 35), Pettit's (mile 39.9), Sunburst Ranch (mile 43.6), Dawt Mill Resort (mile ~47) — plus USFS campgrounds at Hammond (mile 29.2), Patrick Bridge (mile 42.1), and Sycamore (near SH 101 / SH 14). Post-2017-flood insurance rules: Dawt Mill allows private boats for registered guests only.
The 2017 flood reshaped both the trout fishery and outfitter infrastructure — a reminder the river is flash-flood capable despite its spring base flow.

accessPoints.name: SH 14 bridge / Twin Bridges
accessPoints.notes: Outfitter hub and the popular start for the lower/main reach; put-in for the north-fork-main section.

accessPoints.name: Hammond / North Fork Recreation Area (NFRA)
accessPoints.notes: USFS day-use $2/veh (annual $20; Ava MTNF 417-683-4428); gate opens Mar 1 day-use / May 15 camping; 20 sites (1 electric), vault toilets, water; Blue Spring Trail 0.5 mi. Hazard: Hwy CC low-water bridge — portage in high water.
accessPoints.source: owner-provided Ozark County access guide + river-guide, 2026-07

accessPoints.name: River of Life Farm
accessPoints.notes: 500-acre trout resort/outfitter (stilted treehouse cabins, fly guides); The Falls is directly in front. See services.

accessPoints.name: The Falls
accessPoints.notes: 3-ft river-wide ledge; run it or portage either bank.

accessPoints.notes: Hwy KK; low-water bridge at mile 39.4; 7.1 ac MDC access; 3 primitive campsites; privy; paved parking. Directions: CR 354 off H Hwy, ~1 mi past Blair Bridge.
accessPoints.source: owner-provided Ozark County access guide + river-guide, 2026-07

accessPoints.notes: Fee campground/outfitter on river right (CR 354, W side). See services.

accessPoints.name: Patrick Bridge
accessPoints.notes: Hwy H; low-water slab; gravel ramp on the NE side; 161 ac MDC access; 13 campsites, 2 pit toilets; Althea Spring (MO's 23rd largest) + old mill-dam waterfall via trail; near Caulfield (4 mi N on Hwy 160). Hazard: portage in high water.
accessPoints.source: owner-provided Ozark County access guide + river-guide, 2026-07

accessPoints.notes: Fee campground/outfitter on river right (CR 352, ~0.25 mi N of Patrick Bridge). See services.

accessPoints.notes: USACE access below US 160; rebuilt post-2017, currently submerged by high lake level (park on the Hwy 160 shoulder and carry). The lower ~2 mi is Norfork Lake backwater — paddle required. Coordinate ≈ the Tecumseh gauge (from-gauge estimate); human should confirm the pin.

openQuestions: DANGEROUS ANCHOR UNSOURCED (dangerous anchor OMITTED, not guessed): no floater 'do-not-float' number exists — moherp OBSERVED tops out at 'High', and the highest logged trip, 1230 cfs, was still rated only 'High' and floated. The way to source a high-water cutoff is a phone call to the 5 North Fork outfitters — especially Sunburst Ranch 417-284-3443 and Dawt Mill Resort 417-284-3540 (also Twin Bridges 417-256-7507, River of Life Farm 417-261-7777, Pettit's 417-284-3290). Until then the badge cannot show 'dangerous' from this gauge. NWS flood 20 ft (TNZM7) is a CROSS-CHECK only (~10x floatable stage), not the anchor.

ACCESS GPS: every access point still needs a human pin — the owner access guide gives directions + river-mile, not lat/lon. Only Tecumseh (≈36.6230,-92.2481, from the gauge) has a coordinate, and even that needs confirmation.
LENGTH CONFLICT: 78 mi (owner river-guide, likely the main floatable stem) vs 109 mi (Wikipedia/agent, likely incl. headwaters); floatable ~50 mi (SH 76 → Tecumseh). Reconcile at sign-off.
The Falls river-mile: 36.9 (owner access table) vs 36 (agent) — reconcile.
Dawt Mill Dam river-mile: 45.6 (owner access table) vs ~47 (owner services note for Dawt Mill Resort) — reconcile.
McKee Bridge (near River of Life Farm): owner says still an occasional high-water obstacle; agent's American Whitewater source said REMOVED Jul 2024 — CONFLICT, verify.
Trout-management-area (Blue Ribbon) boundaries post-2017 flood — not pinned down.

Reach division: the main/lower reach SH 14 → Tecumseh is calibrated here; the upper reach (SH 76 → SH 14) reads thinner because the Tecumseh gauge over-reads it, and the headwaters above SH 76 are remote/strainer-heavy. Only the main reach carries the badge calibration.
Bryant Creek near Tecumseh (07058000) is a SEPARATE tributary with its own float community; it joins the North Fork at The Forks and is intentionally NOT in gauges[] for this river.
toVerify: Whole-river NHD Permanent Identifier for the North Fork flowline (National Map NHDPlus_HR, MO HUC 11010006). Known at the Tecumseh gauge: NHD ComID 7650991 / reachcode 11010006000122; whole-river PID still UNKNOWN. (GNIS 77861.)
USGS 07057500 (North Fork River near Tecumseh): site #, name, lat/lon, params, drainage area transcribed from the monitoring-location page → verified-identifiers-north-fork-white.md (written).
Full access-point list with coordinates — a human places every point (owner guide gives river-miles + directions only; only Tecumseh has a from-gauge coordinate).
